key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and functional shape-memory behavior after annealing, despite a heterogeneous microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This enables durable NiTi surface coatings for smart actuation and functional protection on engineering components, offering an alternative to brittle ceramic coatings.
method: High velocity air fuel (HVAF) thermal spray of NiTi onto mild steel
Four processing-parameter sets to tune coating quality
Post-processing evaluation of composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
evidence_signal: The coatings were assessed by microstructural and phase-characterization tests plus functional checks including cooling-induced transformation, 3-point bending thermal actuation, nanoindentation, and scratch-based shape-memory response.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings require annealing, and their inhomogeneity, oxides, voids, and limited tensile strength may constrain reliability and scalability.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
